title: Mutton Rogan Josh
description: Rogan Josh is a signature dish of Kashmiri cuisine, celebrated for its deep red hue, aromatic spices, and rich flavor. Traditionally slow-cooked, it balances tender mutton with layers of spices and yogurt for a velvety, luxurious curry.

ingredients.title: For the Mutton
items: Mutton (goat/lamb, bone-in preferred) – 1 kg, medium-sized pieces
Mustard oil – 120 ml (or ghee for a richer version)

ingredients.title: Creaminess & Balance
items: Onion paste – 2 medium onions, browned and blended (optional)

ingredients.title: Seasoning & Finishing
items: Warm water or mutton stock – 600–700 ml
Fresh coriander leaves – small handful, chopped
Saffron strands – 8–10, soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk (optional)

instruction.title: 1. Prepare the Mutton
steps: Wash and pat the mutton pieces dry.
Heat mustard oil until it reaches smoking point, then reduce the heat.
Fry the mutton in batches until browned on all sides.
Remove and set aside.

instruction.title: 2. Infuse the Spices
steps: In the same oil, add green cardamoms, black cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, and bay leaves.
Sauté until fragrant.
Add asafoetida (hing).
Mix Kashmiri red chili powder with 2 tbsp warm water and stir it into the oil to prevent burning.

instruction.title: 3. Build the Gravy
steps: Return the browned mutton to the pan and coat well with the spiced oil.
Add fennel powder, dry ginger powder, coriander powder, turmeric, and salt.
Reduce heat to low.
Gradually add the whisked yogurt while stirring continuously to prevent splitting.
If using, stir in the browned onion paste for a richer, silkier gravy.

instruction.title: 4. Slow Cook
steps: Pour in the hot water or mutton stock.
Cover and simmer on low heat for 60–90 minutes until the mutton is tender.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Adjust with a little more stock or water if needed to achieve a medium-thick consistency.

instruction.title: 5. Finish
steps: Stir in the garam masala.
Add the saffron-infused milk if using.
Simmer for another 5 minutes.
Allow the curry to rest for 15 minutes before serving.
Garnish lightly with chopped fresh coriander leaves.

chefTips: Bone-in mutton produces the richest and most flavorful curry.
Mustard oil gives the authentic Kashmiri flavor; ghee offers a richer restaurant-style version.
Always dissolve Kashmiri chili powder in warm water before adding to prevent scorching.
Add yogurt gradually over low heat while stirring continuously to avoid curdling.
Slow cooking is essential for tender meat and deep flavor development.
The curry is ready when the oil separates and rises to the surface.
Serve with steamed basmati rice, naan, sheermal, or saffron pulao.
Avoid adding cream to maintain the authentic flavor profile.
